transliteration:
divya-mālyāmbara-dharaṃ divya-gandhānulepanam
sarvāścarya-mayaṃ devam anantaṃ viśvato-mukham
literal_meaning: divine-garments-and-flowers-wearing / divine-sandal-paste-anointed / all-wonder-filled / shining-being / endless / facing-everywhere
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s psyche is flooded by a trans-personal vision: every perceptual channel simultaneously overflows with numinous imagery, dissolving the boundary between self and cosmos. The ego’s usual filters are removed, revealing that identity is not confined to a single body-mind but is an open, boundless field of awareness.
psychological_state: Peak/numinous experience: ego boundaries temporarily dissolve, producing simultaneous ecstasy and dread; the psyche registers its own infinitude.
trigger_scenario: A first high-dose psychedelic session, a near-death experience, or an unexpected moment of oceanic oneness during deep meditation where the usual sense of a separate 'I' disappears and everything appears alive, conscious, and interconnected.
anchor_text: You are the limitless awareness in which all experiences appear and dissolve.
